zotero.abstractNote: The Nadaraya-Watson estimator is a well-known non-parametric method for regression. While its asymptotic properties are well-studied, finite-sample bounds are often loose or require strong assumptions. In this paper, we derive a new upper bound on the bias of the Nadaraya-Watson estimator under the assumption that the regression function is Lipschitz continuous. We show that this bound is tighter than existing ones and holds for a wide range of kernel functions. We also provide a discussion on the implications of our result for the choice of the bandwidth parameter.
